summary:
The following are the key takeaways from Apple's earnings conference call:
- Q4 FY2025 revenue reached $102.5 billion, up 8% YoY, setting a September quarter record with EPS of $1.85 (up 13% adjusted)
- December quarter guidance projects 10-12% YoY revenue growth, which would be Apple's best quarter ever and best iPhone quarter ever with double-digit iPhone growth
- Services revenue hit an all-time record of $28.8 billion (up 15% YoY) and fiscal year 2025 Services exceeded $100 billion for the first time
- iPhone revenue was $49 billion (up 6% YoY) with supply constraints on several iPhone 16 and iPhone 17 models due to strong demand
- Greater China revenue declined 4% YoY primarily due to supply constraints, but management expects return to growth in Q1 with store traffic up significantly
- Gross margin of 47.2% exceeded guidance despite $1.1 billion in tariff-related costs; December quarter gross margin guided at 47-48%
- Mac revenue grew 13% YoY to $8.7 billion, with nearly half of Mac purchasers being new to the product
- Installed base of active devices reached all-time highs across all product categories and geographic segments
- Apple announced $600 billion U.S. investment over 4 years focused on advanced manufacturing, silicon engineering, and AI; new AI server factory in Houston began production
- Tariffs on China reduced from 20% to 10%, contributing to lower-than-expected tariff impact of $1.4 billion in December quarter guidance
